facebook.content:
"Your identity in Christ is not something you find, but something you confess. When you're uncertain about who you are or what you're called to, start with this: 'I am a child of God, chosen and beloved.' 

As the Bible says, 'I will not forget you. See, I have inscribed you on the palms of my hands; your walls are continually before me.' (Isaiah 49:15-16)

What are the lies you've been believing about yourself? What are you choosing to confess about your identity today?"
facebook.fullPost:
"Your identity in Christ is not something you find, but something you confess. When you're uncertain about who you are or what you're called to, start with this: 'I am a child of God, chosen and beloved.' 

As the Bible says, 'I will not forget you. See, I have inscribed you on the palms of my hands; your walls are continually before me.' (Isaiah 49:15-16)

What are the lies you've been believing about yourself? What are you choosing to confess about your identity today?"

Watch the full teaching

#BibleTeaching #IncreasingFaith #KingdomTeaching

instagram.content:
"When we give our spouse permission to bless us, we open ourselves to the fullness of God's provision. 
In the same way, Abraham gave Sarah full permission to bless him, and God honored that trust.  
Genesis 18:12-15
Can you think of an area in your life where you're withholding permission for God's blessing to flow? Double tap if you're ready to give that permission today."

tiktok.content:
"You're not responsible to fix your marriage, but you are responsible to fix your part in it."

Scripture says, "Love covers a multitude of sins." (1 Peter 4:8) 

What are the areas in your marriage where you're choosing to love and cover, rather than trying to fix or change the other person?
